topic: JetBrains AI Developer Survey April 2026: Key Findings for Dev Teams

key_points: Primary source: JetBrains AI Pulse survey of 10,000+ professional developers
GitHub Copilot: 76% awareness, 29% work usage — still leader in large enterprises (40% at companies with 5,000+ employees)
Claude Code: 57% awareness in Jan 2026 (up from 49% in Sep 2025 and 31% in Apr–Jun 2025), 18% work usage (24% in US/Canada)
Cursor: 69% awareness, 18% work usage — highest conversion rate at 35.5%
90% of developers regularly used at least one AI tool at work (January 2026)
74% had adopted specialized AI tools for developers
Claude Code has highest product loyalty: 91% CSAT and 54 NPS
JetBrains HAX Study: analyzed 2 years of log data from 800 software developers (400 AI users, 400 non-users)
AI users increased characters typed by ~600/month vs 75 for non-users
AI users increased code deletions by ~100/month vs 7 for non-users
Over 80% reported AI increased productivity, 50% say coding time decreased
Perception vs reality gap: AI redistributes and reshapes workflows in ways developers don't perceive
No significant change in debugging behavior despite quality perception improvements
Context switching slightly increases despite AI marketing promises
1/5 of accepted AI code later deleted; 7% of accepted code heavily rewritten
70% of developer time spent on comprehension activities (reading, navigating, reviewing)
Conversion rate analysis: Cursor 35.5%, GitHub Copilot 23.7%, Antigravity 17.1%
Google Antigravity: 120% YoY awareness growth (fastest growth rate of any tool)
Three drivers of Antigravity growth: Google ecosystem, free preview strategy, multi-agent differentiation
Multi-agent parallel processing capability highlighted as unique differentiator
Reached 6% adoption rate by January 2026 since November 2025 launch
84% of developers use or plan to use AI tools (up from 76% in 2024)
Only 29% of developers trust AI outputs — down from 40% in 2024
51% of professional developers now use AI tools daily
GitHub Copilot: ~20M total users, 4.7M paid subscribers (Jan 2026)
Cursor: $2B+ ARR (Feb 2026), $29.3B valuation, 1M+ daily active users
Nearly 9 in 10 developers who use AI save at least 1 hour/week
Code churn rose from 3.1% in 2020 to 5.7% in 2024
Code duplication increased from 8.3% to 12.3% of changed lines (2021–2024)
JetBrains AI tools reached six-figure active paid user count in Q4 2025
240% YoY growth in AI tool adoption (Q4 2024–Q4 2025)
Junie (JetBrains AI coding agent): 343K users, 1M+ AI-assisted actions
83% of managers believe Junie increases team productivity
12.5M+ recurring active users across all JetBrains tools
25.69% YoY revenue growth
88 Fortune Global Top 100 companies use JetBrains tools

stats.fact: 90% of developers regularly used at least one AI tool at work (January 2026)
74% of developers worldwide had adopted specialized AI tools for developers by January 2026
GitHub Copilot: 76% awareness, 29% work usage globally; 40% usage at companies with 5,000+ employees
Claude Code: 57% awareness in Jan 2026 (up from 31% in Apr–Jun 2025), 18% work usage globally, 24% in US/Canada
Claude Code has highest product loyalty metrics: 91% CSAT and 54 NPS
Cursor: 69% awareness, 18% work usage, 35.5% conversion rate (highest of all tools measured)
stats.source: JetBrains AI Pulse Survey + Antigravity Lab analysis

stats.fact: AI users increased code characters typed by 600/month vs 75 for non-users
stats.source: JetBrains HAX Study (800 developers, 2-year log analysis)

stats.fact: AI users increased code deletions by ~100/month vs 7 for non-users — behavior developers don't perceive
Experienced developers were 19% slower with AI tools despite perceiving themselves as 20% faster
stats.source: METR study (cited in JetBrains AI Pulse report)

stats.fact: Over 80% of developers reported AI slightly or significantly increased their productivity
50% reported coding time decreased after AI adoption; 15% said it increased
1/5 of accepted AI code is later deleted; 7% of accepted code is heavily rewritten
43.5% reported code quality increased; 6.5% said it decreased; 50% saw no change
81% of developers are concerned about security and data privacy when using AI agents
Code churn rose from 3.1% (2020) to 5.7% (2024); code duplication from 8.3% to 12.3% (2021–2024)
OpenAI GPT models most-used LLMs among developers (81%); Claude Sonnet used by 45% of professional devs and has highest 'admired' rating at 67.5%
stats.source: JetBrains Developer Ecosystem Survey 2026 preview data

stats.fact: JetBrains Junie: 343K users, 1M+ AI-assisted actions; 83% of managers say it increases team productivity
Cursor: $2B+ ARR (Feb 2026), $29.3B valuation, 1M+ daily active users; enterprise = 60% of revenue
Only 29% of developers trust AI outputs to be accurate — down from 40% in 2024
Nearly 9 in 10 developers who use AI save at least 1 hour per week; 1 in 5 saves 8+ hours

angles: The Productivity Paradox: 80%+ of devs feel more productive, yet studies show 19% slower task completion — what's really happening
Claude Code's explosive rise: from 3% to 18% work usage in under a year, now tied with Cursor for #2 spot
Enterprise AI strategy decisions: which tools are actually winning in large organizations (5,000+ employees) and why
The hidden cost of AI code acceptance: 1 in 5 AI-suggested lines later gets deleted — measuring the real rework burden
Security trade-off: 81% of developers worried about AI agents and data privacy — what enterprise teams need to know
Behavior change without awareness: JetBrains HAX study reveals devs edit 100x more code than they realize when using AI
Tool fragmentation: most developers use 3+ AI tools — implications for team standardization and licensing decisions
JetBrains as a credible data source: how their methodology (10K+ devs, longitudinal log data) compares to other surveys
keywords: best AI coding tools for teams 2026
outline: H2: JetBrains April 2026 Survey: Methodology and Why It Matters
H2: Overall AI Adoption: 90% of Developers Now Use AI Tools at Work
H2: Market Share Breakdown: GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, Cursor, and the Rising Challengers
H2: The Productivity Paradox: What Developers Perceive vs. What the Data Shows
H2: Code Quality Trade-offs: Increases, Decreases, and Hidden Rework Costs
H2: Enterprise Considerations: Security, Trust, and Procurement Patterns
